title: Drag&drop fails almost every time when I try to attach a file
excerpt: Hi. I experience this daily myself, and also I heard my colleague have the same issue. when I drag a file to attach it to an update or a comment in a Monday item, the area where I need to “drop” the file begins flickerin…

posts.author: ArtemS (Artem)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi. I experience this daily myself, and also I heard my colleague have the same issue. when I drag a file to attach it to an update or a comment in a Monday item, the area where I need to “drop” the file begins flickering even if I don’t move the mouse. I try to drop it anyway, and it fails. See video to understand better what I mean: 11.03.2026_18.03.08_REC Browser is Edge. Other colleague have the same issue on Chrome.

posts.author: YOGESHP (Yogesh Pokale)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @ArtemS, Welcome to the community!

Since this is happening in both Edge and Chrome, and multiple users are experiencing it, it’s unlikely to be browser-specific.

As a first step, try testing in an Incognito/InPrivate window to rule out any extension interference. If the flickering still occurs, it’s likely an account-level issue rather than something local to the browser.

I’d recommend reaching out to monday.com Support and attaching the screen recording. Be sure to mention that it’s happening across different browsers and users that context will help them investigate more effectively.

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
That definitely sounds frustrating — especially if it’s happening consistently.

A couple things worth checking since drag-and-drop behavior can sometimes be affected by the browser environment:

Have you tried:

• Disabling browser extensions temporarily, if you can (especially ad blockers, screen recorders, or security tools). Those can sometimes interfere with drag events and cause the flickering you described.
• Testing in an Incognito/Private window to rule out extension conflicts.
• Hard refreshing the page (Ctrl + Shift + R) or clearing cache.

What’s not normal is the flickering drop zone in your video. That usually indicates one of a few things:

• The browser repeatedly losing and regaining the drag target
• A UI rendering issue
• A browser extension interfering with drag events

Also curious — does this happen on all boards, or only on one specific board? That can help determine whether it’s a board-specific issue or something broader with the browser/UI.

If the flickering happens across boards and browsers, it would probably be worth sending that recording to monday Support so they can take a closer look. The video you shared is actually really helpful for diagnosing issues like this.

posts.author: Greatnessflows (Philip)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks for reporting this. The flickering drop zone when dragging files is a known issue that can sometimes happen due to browser extensions or hardware acceleration conflicts.

Could you try:
• Disabling browser extensions (especially ad blockers)
• Turning off hardware acceleration in the browser settings
• Trying the same action in an incognito/private window

If the issue persists, please share the screen recording and confirm your OS version so the team can investigate further.

posts.author: taylorbrooks (Taylor Brooks)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @ArtemS,

I have seen this exact flickering behavior in other web apps that use complex drag-and-drop handlers. A few additional things to try:

1. Check your mouse DPI/settings

High-DPI gaming mice or aggressive mouse acceleration can sometimes cause the browser to register micro-movements that trigger the flickering drop zone. Try lowering your mouse polling rate temporarily.

2. Test with a different mouse

If you have a spare mouse (even a basic one), test with that. I have seen cases where specific mouse drivers conflict with browser drag events.

3. Check for touch screen interference

If your device has a touch screen, try disabling it temporarily. Sometimes touch + mouse input creates conflicting drag events.

4. Browser zoom level

Try resetting your browser zoom to 100% (Ctrl+0). Non-standard zoom levels can sometimes break drag target calculations.

The fact that it is happening across Edge and Chrome suggests it is either hardware-related (mouse) or account-level as others mentioned. The video is definitely helpful - that rapid flickering is not normal behavior.

Worth opening a support ticket with that recording if none of the above helps!

posts.author: ArtemS (Artem)
posts.cookedHtml:
@ProjectMastermind @Greatnessflows @taylorbrooks Thank you all for your comments. I disabled all the browser extension I had on, turned off hardware acceleration, hard refreshed the page and tried to set a lower DPI setting on my mouse - none of it helped.

Then I logged in in InPrivate mode, and flickering was gone. It became clear to me that the issue is probably with extensions. Turns out it was not enough to “disable” them. I had to remove them to get rid of the flickering. And by the way the problematic extension was not the adblocker one. It was most likely Tampermonkey. Anyhow, I removed that and a few other extensions which were not crucial for me, and now drag&drop attachments work just fine. Thank you all!

posts.author: edarling85 (Wild 6 | Eric Darling)
posts.cookedHtml:
First, try doing a hard refresh of the page (Ctrl + Shift + R). Sometimes the board interface gets stuck and a full refresh resolves it.

Second, check if any browser extensions are interfering. Extensions like ad blockers, privacy tools, or UI modifiers can sometimes break drag-and-drop behavior. Opening the board in an incognito/private window is a quick way to test this.

Third, clearing your browser cache and cookies can help if cached scripts are causing the flickering in the updates section.

You could also try uploading the file using the upload button instead of dragging it. If that works normally, it usually confirms the issue is specifically with the drag-and-drop interaction.

Finally, check whether the problem happens on multiple boards or just one. If it’s consistent across browsers and boards, it might be worth sending the screen recording to monday.com support so they can investigate whether it’s a UI bug.

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml: Happy to help!
